comparability:
QUANTIFIED DECOMPOSITION OF THE "F1 GAP". Nominal gap: project numbers quoted as 0.75-0.81 vs literature 0.85-0.95, midpoint gap ~12pp. Three corrections must be applied before treating any of it as model deficit.

(1) The project's quoted band conflates three different metrics (eval auditor): the 0.800 unified_reviewall_A headline is PIXEL-level set-theoretic area F1 on 5 JHB val grids at a swept operating point, NOT polygon F1@0.5; the 0.709 CT independent_26 number is polygon PRESENCE F1 at IoU>=0.1 with pred-side many-to-one merge; Ch1 0.749 is human-review precision, not IoU-based. Honest polygon-level@IoU0.5 numbers: recall 68.8% (V3-C+SAM) to 79.6% (train20 per-det+SAM) on the JHB 25-grid clean_gt; strict-1:1 polygon F1 cross-domain 0.36 (vs 0.763 area F1 on the SAME predictions — i.e., the strict polygon number is dominated by GT carving, not detection failure).

(2) The literature's 0.85-0.95 is metrically incommensurable (both lit auditors agree): those are pixel-semantic F1/IoU on curated, PV-dense, in-domain chip datasets (USGS-CA 0.94, PV01 0.91, Heilbronn 0.96) or image-level classification (DeepSolar P93/R89). Essentially NO published rooftop-PV system reports instance/polygon F1@IoU0.5. The closest published object-level anchor is SolarMapper array-wise AP@IoU0.5 = 0.71-0.82 (Duke, clean conditions); the only published Mask R-CNN instance-level PV delineation F1 is ~0.73 (satellite)/0.68 (UAV). On true aerial orthophotos — ZAsolar's actual regime — published SOTA pixel F1 collapses to 0.74 (S3Former on IGN; all baselines IoU 0.39-0.59) and SolarDK's best pixel IoU is 0.72. ZAsolar's matched-metric pixel area_F1 of 0.80-0.85 in-domain is therefore AT OR ABOVE the aerial-orthophoto literature, and its polygon@0.5 performance sits inside the SolarMapper 0.71-0.82 object-AP band. Cross-domain, literature drops are WORSE than ZAsolar's (classification F1 0.98->0.46 cross-provider; DE->DK zero-shot 0.238; ZAsolar keeps bulk 1.04/R2 0.97 aggregate).

(3) GT noise/fragmentation caps measurable polygon F1 on the project's own suites: GT is 100% A2 SAM-drawn sub-array polygons (zero freehand installation-level gold anywhere), ~1.49 fragments per installation, matched-pair mean IoU only 0.73-0.74 (so boundary noise pushes true matches below the 0.5 IoU bar), clean_gt embeds accepted V3-C predictions (circularity), and literature quantifies GT-noise effects of this magnitude (SolarMapper audit moved precision 0.45->0.67; 42% of 'FPs' were correct; BDAPPV pre-consensus area correlation 0.61-0.68; humans miss ~3.8% of visible panels). Estimated measurable ceiling on current A2 GT: ~0.85-0.88 polygon F1@0.5 (merge profile) even for a near-perfect model.

ATTRIBUTION: of the ~12pp nominal gap, roughly 7-9pp (60-75%) is metric-type + benchmark-population non-comparability plus the GT-noise/fragmentation ceiling, and roughly 4-6pp (30-40%) is real, measured, addressable model deficit. The real deficit is concretely localized: (a) sub-array recall on multi-array roofs 0.30@IoU0.5 (Ch2 SAM_supp class), mid-size 30-100m2 recall 0.33, with G0925-class misses proven detector-bounded (proposal-stage, not postproc); (b) precision collapse confined to the s_xs x c_lo stratum (0.286 vs 0.78-1.00 elsewhere; 77% of small FPs are water heaters in the 5-20m2 band); (c) boundary halo (raw bulk 1.17) costing near-miss IoU conversions; (d) cross-domain per-grid dispersion (sigma_Bw 8x) from lookalike over-paint + dense-roof recall, with aggregate calibration intact.

REALISTIC TARGETS: in-domain polygon F1@0.5 under the installation (merge) profile against current A2 GT: 0.80-0.83 is achievable with the levers below; ~0.85-0.88 is the GT ceiling — chasing 0.90+ on current GT is chasing a metric artifact. Strict-1:1 polygon F1@0.5 in-domain: 0.72-0.78. On a new T1 installation-level gold suite (candidate C10), 0.85+ is plausible and would be the honest literature-comparable number alongside the pixel area_F1 0.80-0.85, which should be benchmarked against SolarMapper's 0.71-0.82 array AP and S3Former's 0.74 IGN-aerial pixel F1 — not against curated-chip 0.9+ numbers. The V1.4 demotion of per-polygon F1 to diagnostic in favor of aggregate calibration is exactly what the national-scale literature (DeepPVMapper, DeepSolar, SolarMapper recommendations) converged on.

candidates.title: Multi-scale + flip TTA at the raw-proposal stage of detect_direct.py
candidates.what: Implement TTA in /home/gaosh/projects/ZAsolar/detect_direct.py (the raw_detections.pkl producer): per 400px chip, run hflip/vflip/rot90 plus 2-3 scales (e.g. 0.9/1.0/1.25 via the model transform min_size or chip resampling), inverse-transform boxes/masks, pool all proposals, and let the existing finalize.py per-detection vectorization + grid-level spatial NMS IoU 0.5 dedup. Gate behind a --tta flag; evaluate on JHB CBD 25 clean_gt (Ch2 recall + Tier-1) and cape_town_independent_26 under BOTH merge modes (constraint g). Protocol: compare against unified_A and V3-C at their locked operating points; re-run poly_conf_sweep since TTA shifts the score distribution.
candidates.why: Recall is the binding constraint on polygon F1 (Ch2 SAM_supp recall 0.30@0.5; mid-size 0.33; poly recall ceiling 68-80%), G0925/PTA-class misses are proven detector-bounded at the proposal stage, and TTA is the ONE sanctioned inference-time recall lever (constraint e) with zero code existing (grep confirmed). New augmented views give the RPN/ROI heads fresh chances at proposals that a single pass misses; scale views specifically attack the dense fine-carved-roof failure (PTA0292 area-R 0.06).
candidates.expected_impact: +2-4pp polygon F1@0.5 in-domain, larger on dense cross-domain grids. Basis: literature TTA gains +1.59 mask mAP / +2.2 AP50 (IJCAI challenge reports, CenterNet-class), gains skew to borderline-recall detections; project-side, even +5-10pp recall on the 0.30 SAM_supp class converts to ~+2pp suite-level F1 at held precision (solar_cls absorbs the FP side in-domain, kill 0.959).
candidates.cost: 2-4 days engineering; inference cost 4-8x (acceptable: census runs are batch jobs; 6 parallel on 5090 already standard); 0 GPU-days training.
candidates.evidence: Internal: memory feedback_recall_recovery_constraints ('TTA the only viable path'); exp_finalizer doc:121-144 (detector-bounded framing); grep tta = empty (verified this session). Literature: arXiv:2010.10957 (+1.59 mask mAP); lit:maskrcnn-upgrades TTA finding.
candidates.risk: FP increase on lookalikes (TTA raises recall of FPs too) — mitigate via solar_cls in-domain and the locked conf sweep; score-distribution shift invalidates existing thresholds (must re-sweep on val grids only, not reporting grids); 4-8x inference cost on the 2214-grid CT census may force selective TTA (dense-grid trigger).

history.reason: Not a rehash: zero TTA code exists (grep verified), no prior multi-scale/TTA experiment under any name, and TTA is the explicitly sanctioned sole remaining inference-time recall lever (constraint e; exp_finalizer doc lines 121/144 say finalizer changes cannot move the needle 'until TTA or a retrained model'). No standing-constraint violations: clean_gt lock respected, Tier-1 metrics invoked, both merge modes covered, no loss/GT changes. Prerequisites verified accurate (detect_direct.py chip 400 / score-thr 0.05 / raw_detections.pkl; finalize.py merge-mode + spatial_nms 0.5). However, the candidate's core mechanism claim ('fresh chances at proposals a single pass misses') contradicts the repo's own raw-hint audit: raw_box_hint_rate is already 0.973-1.000 at score 0.05 on G0816/G0817/G0925, so proposal-EXISTENCE headroom is ~3%; the binding detector-bounded failure is mask undersizing / proposal IoU quality. Flip views give correlated errors; only scale views attack the documented failure. Committing 2-4 days + a full threshold re-sweep before a cheap mechanism probe repeats the anti-pattern the NMS-relax probe (e8e2590, half a day, killed the lever) was designed to prevent.
history.modification: Gate the full build behind a half-day probe and fix the mechanism framing: (1) Before any inverse-mask plumbing or re-sweep, run detect_direct.py forward passes on TTA views of G0925 + one PTA dense grid only — prioritize scale views (e.g. 1.25x/1.5x and 0.9x via chip resampling or transform min_size), flips secondary — inverse-transform boxes/masks per-view, and re-run the existing exp_finalizer raw-hint audit measuring per-missed-polygon BEST-PROPOSAL IoU (mask hint at IoU 0.3/0.5), not hint existence (existence is already 97-100% single-pass). Pass bar: TTA views convert >=10-15% of currently-missed SAM_supp polygons into IoU>=0.5-recoverable proposals; otherwise stop at probe cost (~0.5 day, not 2-4 days). (2) Rewrite 'why' to claim scale-driven mask-quality/score recovery rather than new-proposal generation. (3) Baseline comparisons must be same-caliber re-runs of v3c/unified_A through the same direct pipeline + finalize config + eval-adapter IoU — never historical presence-F1@0.1 numbers (known cross-pipeline confound: 0.1 vs 0.3 IoU, hardcoded CONF_TIERED). (4) Record TTA params in the raw artifact manifest for provenance, and watch the bulk gate under pixel-or specifically: pooled near-duplicate masks from misaligned inverse transforms fatten the OR-union before NMS ever runs (NMS dedup only protects per-detection mode).

evidence.reason: The candidate's expected impact (+2-4pp polygon F1) does not survive evidence transfer, for a project-specific reason the candidate missed: train.py TrainTransforms (verified, lines 243-330) already trains every production checkpoint with random hflip/vflip/rot90 AND 0.8-1.2x scale jitter — i.e., the proposed TTA view set (hflip/vflip/rot90 + 0.9/1.25 scales) lies almost entirely inside the model's trained equivariance envelope, where TTA views yield correlated outputs and near-zero novel proposals. The cited gains (+1.59 mask mAP IJCAI challenge, +2.2 AP50 CenterNet) are (a) integral PR-curve metrics whose TTA gains accrue as low-confidence tail detections that die at the locked c=0.925 operating point unless the threshold drops, and (b) measured on curated/natural-image data with no train-aug overlap stated. The targeted failures are partly outside TTA's mechanism anyway: exp_finalizer doc (verified) attributes G0925 to mask-undersizing + no-box-hint (mask-head problem), and PTA0292 area-R 0.06 is a domain collapse a 0.9-1.25 scale wobble cannot recover. The cross-domain upside claim is the least supported: solar_cls kill=0.959 is in-domain CT only and Gemini FP-review is red-lit cross-domain (-24pp), so TTA's FP-recall increase has no absorber exactly where the candidate claims the larger gain. Cost is also undercounted: beyond 4-8x inference on a 2214-grid census already OOM-capped at PAR=3, the full recalibration cascade (poly_conf_sweep, per-layer solar_cls thresholds calibrated on non-TTA score distributions, SAM rerun, both merge modes x two suites) is 1-2 weeks, not 2-4 days. KEEP the core because it is the only sanctioned untried inference-time recall lever (memory + exp_finalizer both verified) and out-of-envelope upscale views retain a real mechanism for small/dense sub-array misses — but only behind a cheap falsification gate and with honest impact numbers.
evidence.modification: Three concrete changes. (1) Replace the view set: drop hflip/vflip/rot90 and the 0.9/1.25 scales (all inside the trained augmentation envelope of train.py TrainTransforms); use only out-of-envelope upscale views, e.g. 1.5x and 2.0x chip resampling (with overlapping-window handling at 400px), which are the only views with a mechanism to fire on currently-missed small/dense sub-arrays. (2) Gate the full 2-4 day integration behind a ~half-day proposal-stage falsification pilot: pre-resample tiles for G0925, G0817, 2-3 lowest-Ch2-recall clean_gt grids (plus PTA0292 if tiles are local), run the existing detect_direct.py unchanged per view, and measure the fraction of currently-missed clean_gt polygons that gain ANY raw proposal at conf >= 0.3 in ANY view. Kill criterion: <10% of missed polygons recover a proposal -> abandon TTA entirely (no merge/inverse-transform engineering, no recalibration cascade). (3) Restate the claim: expected impact +0.5-1.5pp polygon F1 in-domain at a re-swept operating point; delete the 'larger on dense cross-domain grids' claim (no FP absorber there); restate cost as 1-2 weeks end-to-end including poly_conf_sweep redo and solar_cls per-imagery-layer threshold recalibration, with inference budgeted at 2-3x (two extra scale views), not 4-8x.

history.reason: The candidate's premise — "the SAM-halo-audit decision was planned, never wired" — is false in substance. The audit's recommended mask-supervision pool (JHB sam_fn_review SAM_added, 1,699 polys) is ALREADY trusted in production unified_reviewall_A: build_unified_reviewall.py:200 maps JHB <grid>_sam_added.gpkg to label_source=sam_added_browser, which boundary_trust_rules.yaml sets to mask_trusted=true/boundary_w=1.0 (confirmed in handoff doc 2026-05-12-unified-reviewall-runpod-handoff.md:12). The audit's excluded pool (V3C_correct→reviewed_prediction) is untrusted, as prescribed. The per-instance mask_trusted gate superseded the audit's area-threshold/stage-ordering design (which had the REVERSE freeze order anyway: mask-BCE first, freeze in Stage 2). The only literally-unwired piece is untrusting the >=600m2 SAM tail: n=15 polygons, which the audit itself flags as wide-CI/"minimal damage". The candidate's actual delta therefore reduces to: (a) sam_refined_review — ZERO instances in the training set (absent from training_sets.yaml source_family and both pool manifests), a no-op; (b) flipping CT sam_added_true_fn (~1.2-1.4k train instances) to trusted — a population the 99.1% audit NEVER covered (it audited JHB interactive browser-SAM on Vexcel; these are non-interactive batch SAM cuts on CT aerial), and which the unified build EXPLICITLY classified untrusted with a dedicated fix commit (22a2b9c2; handoff line 277: "CT sam_fn_marker 和 sam_fn_review 都是非交互式 batch SAM cut → untrusted, 已修"), with the sanctioned alternative recorded at handoff line 326 (interactively re-cut ~250 → sam_added_browser, or add new H labels). The impact math is also wrong: "mask supervision grows 2.5x (3.4k→9k)" is impossible without flipping reviewed_prediction (6,591 of the 8,270 untrusted pool), which the candidate doesn't propose and the audit contraindicates (V3C halo bias = train20's +49pp bulk root cause); real growth is <=1.36x, composed entirely of unaudited masks. Cited prerequisite ab28330 is per-CHIP max_polygon_area_m2 (built for solar_zerov2 scale_jitter routing), not the per-instance area needed. What survives is a different, far smaller candidate — re-run scripts/analysis/audit_sam_supp_size_reliability.py on the CT sam_added_true_fn population first, or execute the handoff's ~250-polygon interactive re-cut — neither of which is this proposal, and neither justifies a 1-2 GPU-day retrain on current evidence.

evidence.reason: Fatal factual error verified in-repo: the candidate's mechanism (grow trusted mask supervision 2.5x with audited-better SAM masks) is built on a pool that does not exist. (1) The halo audit's winners (sam_added_browser) are ALREADY mask_trusted=true/boundary_w=1.0 in production unified_reviewall_A (data/training_pool/boundary_trust_rules.yaml:41, build_unified_reviewall.py:21, --per-instance-mask-trusted ON per training_sets.yaml) — the 'never wired' grep finding misreads history: the 2026-05-08 600m² staged-pool decision was superseded 2026-05-11 by the binary per-source trust gate that ships the same audited pool. (2) Measured composition of the untrusted ~5.6k: CT corpus has reviewed_prediction=7,393, sam_added_true_fn=184, sam_refined_review=0; JHB untrusted is all reviewed_prediction — i.e. ~95% are V3C_correct masks, the audit's documented LOSERS (99.1%) and the exact halo source the JHB Phase A failure (bulk 1.897) proved gets learned under pixel-BCE. The candidate's own cited evidence argues against trusting this pool. (3) Under its stated flip rule (sam_added_* + sam_refined ≤600m²) the trusted pool grows only +184 instances (~5%, not 3.4k→9k), and those 184 are non-interactive batch SAM cuts explicitly classified in the builder as boundary noise, on CT aerial — the audit (interactive browser SAM, JHB Vexcel) does not cover that tool or imagery. The +1-2pp F1 / bulk -0.05-0.15 expected impact has no surviving evidential basis, and no modification preserves the mechanism: the audited-better masks are in, the rest are audited-worse, and a 184-instance flip cannot justify a 1-2 GPU-day retrain.

candidates.title: Ignore regions that reach RPN/box-cls loss (iscrowd-style) + standalone area-adaptive boundary ignore band
candidates.what: Two orthogonal pieces, landed as separate commits: (a) plumb a region/instance-level ignore through CocoSolarDataset (train.py currently hardcodes iscrowd=0, verified lines 209/223/615) so ambiguous/unlabeled-PV regions are EXCLUDED from RPN objectness and ROI-cls negatives (Detectron2-standard semantics) — populate ignore regions from FN-marker corpora, Gemini-abstain zones, and unreviewed-chip margins; (b) implement supervision-layering action 5 as the planned standalone module core/training/boundary_ignore_band.py with area-adaptive band widths (small 1px / medium 2px / large+S 3px) replacing the current fixed band_iters=2 — explicitly NOT bundled with boundary-aware loss.
candidates.why: Today an unlabeled real panel inside a training chip is supervised as HARD BACKGROUND in RPN and cls loss — directly teaching the detector to suppress real PV. The project's own history proves training chips contained unlabeled positives (entire sam_added_true_fn corpora exist = panels that were missed at annotation time). This is a credible root cause of the multi-array sub-array FN problem (1/20 detected). The ignore-band half is the formally-open orthogonal lever from the Phase A postmortem (constraint: 'never ablated').
candidates.expected_impact: +1-3pp recall-driven polygon F1. Basis: ignore-region handling is standard in every Detectron2/MMDet-era strong baseline for incompletely-labeled data; no direct pp citation exists for PV, so treat as mechanism-strong/magnitude-uncertain — which is exactly why it must be a single-lever commit with its own A/B.
candidates.cost: ~1 week engineering (COCO ignore field + loss masking in the patched maskrcnn_loss path + dataset plumbing) + 1 retrain; ignore-region source data already exists (fn_markers, review CSVs).
candidates.evidence: train.py iscrowd hardcoded 0 (verified this session); core/training/boundary_aware_mask.py:1-36 ('still contributes to box + cls'); docs/plans/2026-05-09-training-supervision-layering.md action 5; memory feedback_boundary_aware_vs_ignore_band (ignore band orthogonal to failed Phase A, untested).
candidates.risk: RESEMBLES REJECTED #8 (Phase A) — material difference: Phase A bundled boundary-aware LOSS WEIGHTING (up-weighting noisy boundaries) with four other levers; this is loss EXCLUSION (down-to-zero on ambiguous regions) landed solo, the form the postmortem explicitly left open. Residual risks: over-ignoring starves negatives and inflates FP (cap ignore area fraction per chip); attribution discipline required (one lever per commit per the Phase A lesson).

history.reason: Half the candidate is the genuinely-open, sanctioned lever (supervision-layering action 5, never ablated, all prerequisite claims verified: no core/training/boundary_ignore_band.py, fixed band_iters=2 at train.py:64-83, label_source plumbing in place) and is not a rehash of rejected #8 (Phase A's ignore was mask-loss-only; boundary_aware_mask.py docstring confirms 'still contributes to box + cls'). But the RPN/cls-ignore half fails verification on three points: (1) its motivating premise is stale — the fn_marker corpora it cites as 'unlabeled panels trained as hard background' are ALREADY ingested as labeled positives (label_source=sam_added_true_fn) in build_unified_reviewall.py:223-245, i.e. the production model unified_reviewall_A was trained with them as positives, and using them as ignore would downgrade known positives; (2) 'Gemini-abstain zones' as an ignore source is built on a fixed artifact — abstains were max_tokens truncation, now 0% (memory project_gemini_fp_review_calibration), and the residual genuine ambiguity (skylights) is a hard-NEGATIVE archetype whose removal from cls negatives violates the HN-breadth constraint (d) in a precision-bottlenecked project; (3) the implementation sketch is wrong-layered — maskrcnn_loss cannot reach RPN objectness/ROI-cls; ignore requires sampling-level intervention (assign_targets_to_anchors / select_training_samples), though train.py:1066-1084 scaffolding (install_fastrcnn_patch, wrap_select_training_samples, currently logging-only) lowers that cost. Success criterion '+1-3pp polygon F1' also conflicts with the Tier-1 metric constraint and omits dual merge-mode eval; '1 retrain' contradicts the candidate's own one-lever-per-commit discipline. No repo supervision change has ever delivered recall end-to-end (train20 −12pp Ch2 recall, phaseA 0.300), so the impact claim must be gated on a measured premise, not assumed.
history.modification: Split and re-gate. (1) PROMOTE part (b) to the lead, standalone commit: implement core/training/boundary_ignore_band.py exactly per action-5 spec (area-adaptive 1/2/3px + R-class band-ignore-core-supervised), replacing fixed band_iters=2; one retrain; judge on the Tier-1 set (sigma_Bw + RMSE primary, bulk in [0.5,2.0] gate, polygon F1 diagnostic only) under BOTH merge modes per the merge-mode constraint, against unified_reviewall_A on the locked clean_gt suite. (2) GATE part (a) behind a cheap measurement first: quantify residual unlabeled-positive mass in the CURRENT training pool (e.g. run the production detector over training chips at high conf and count high-confidence detections with no overlapping label outside known-negative chips); proceed only if material (e.g. >2-3% of chips affected). If it proceeds: (i) drop fn_markers as an ignore source — any known-real unlabeled panel goes in as a labeled sam_added-style positive per the established pattern, never as ignore; (ii) drop Gemini-abstain zones entirely (corpus is a fixed max_tokens artifact at ~0%); RA-confirmed lookalikes (skylights/heaters) go to data/negative_pool/, never to ignore; ignore is reserved for regions no reviewer has adjudicated (unreviewed-chip margins), with a per-chip ignore-area cap; (iii) implement at the sampling layer by extending the existing wrap_select_training_samples (ROI) plus a new RPN assign_targets_to_anchors wrapper with label=-1 exclusion semantics — not via maskrcnn_loss; (iv) budget 2 retrains total (one per lever) and the same Tier-1 dual-merge-mode A/B.

evidence.reason: The candidate's own admission ("no direct pp citation exists for PV") is worse than stated: the supplied literature reports contain zero evidence on ignore regions, and the relevant sparse-annotation detection literature shows gains conditional on high (30-50%) missing-label rates that vanish at low rates. The premise that unlabeled positives sit in training chips as hard background is partially stale — the sam_added_true_fn corpora cited as proof were already converted to positive labels via the Ch2 clean-GT build and the 2026-05-13 review-all corpus behind the current production model; the residual missing rate is unmeasured, so +1-3pp has no transferable basis. Two further verified defects: (i) torchvision 0.25 (checked in the project venv) consumes iscrowd nowhere — RPN objectness and ROI-cls negative exclusion require two new gradient-affecting patch points (assign_targets_to_anchors / assign_targets_to_proposals), not the existing patched maskrcnn_loss path the candidate names, so the 1-week cost understates the real surface; (ii) the proposed ignore sources are structurally wrong — Gemini-abstain zones are prediction-conditioned and cannot cover panels the detector also missed (the exact population the mechanism targets), and fn_markers are confirmed positives that should become labels, not ignores. Half (b) is the genuinely sanctioned open ablation (plan action 5, feedback_boundary_aware_vs_ignore_band) but is mask-BCE-only and lands on area metrics (bulk_ratio/σ_Bw), not recall-driven polygon F1@0.5 — it cannot support the headline claim. Mechanism real, scoping and evidence basis not; survives only with a measurement gate.
evidence.modification: Restructure into a gated, two-track item: (1) Add a Phase 0 premise-measurement gate (~2 days, no retrain) before any implementation of half (a): sample 150-200 chips from the CURRENT production training pool, run the existing detector at low confidence plus human/Gemini audit of background regions, and measure the unlabeled-real-PV-as-background rate. Proceed with (a) only if clearly-real unlabeled PV appears in >=5% of chips; otherwise kill (a). The audit output itself becomes the ignore-region corpus (plus genuinely ambiguous PV-vs-thermal regions) — drop Gemini-abstain zones as a source (prediction-conditioned, blind to undetected positives) and convert confirmed fn_markers to positive labels rather than ignores. (2) Correct the engineering plan and budget: implement ignore via new patch points at RPN.assign_targets_to_anchors and RoIHeads.assign_targets_to_proposals (label -1 for anchors/proposals with high intersection-over-foreground against ignore regions), keeping the per-chip ignore-area-fraction cap; the maskrcnn_loss path is irrelevant to RPN/cls negatives. (3) Split half (b) (area-adaptive boundary_ignore_band) into a separate candidate/commit with bulk_ratio/sigma_Bw/area_F1 as its success criteria — it must not book any polygon-F1 recall impact. (4) Re-scope expected impact of (a) to '0 to +2pp polygon F1, conditional on measured missing rate'; the A/B must run dual merge modes on locked clean_gt per project evaluation rules.

candidates.title: Copy-paste augmentation wired to the negative pool (PV-on-lookalike-roof compositing)
candidates.what: Add instance copy-paste to TrainTransforms in /home/gaosh/projects/ZAsolar/train.py (currently flips+rot90+mild jitter only, grep verified no copy-paste/mosaic): paste trusted PV instances (with masks) onto (i) verified-negative roof chips from the negative pool and (ii) existing positive chips to synthesize dense multi-array configurations; match GSD per imagery layer, simple alpha-blend edges, keep paste count ~1-4/chip at p~0.3. Ablate as its own retrain vs C2's baseline.
candidates.why: Attacks the two measured deficits simultaneously: dense multi-array recall (synthesizes the rare many-sub-arrays-per-roof configurations the detector fails at, 0.30 recall class) and lookalike-FP discrimination (model sees PV and skylights/heaters in the SAME chip context, forcing contrastive features rather than context shortcuts). It is the single best-documented augmentation for small-object instance segmentation and is absent from this repo.
candidates.expected_impact: +1-2pp polygon F1. Basis: Ghiasi CVPR 2021 +0.6-1.5 AP even on a 49-AP baseline (more in data-scarce regimes — ZAsolar's 8.5k positive chips qualifies); Kisantal +9.7% relative on small-object instance segmentation with Mask R-CNN specifically.
candidates.cost: ~1 week engineering (paste realism on orthoimagery: GSD scaling, rotation, no-overlap constraints) + 1 retrain; reuses negative-pool chips from C2.
candidates.evidence: train.py:244-385 TrainTransforms (no copy-paste, verified); arXiv:2012.07177 (Ghiasi); arXiv:1902.07296 (Kisantal); memory project_ch1_fragmentation (multi-array recall hole).
candidates.risk: Paste artifacts (shadow-direction mismatch, edge halos) could teach 'pasted-looking = PV' shortcuts — mitigate with blending and by including pasted NEGATIVE objects (heaters onto roofs) symmetrically; interacts with mask-trust gating (pasted instances must carry trusted masks, so source only from the trusted 3.4k pool).

history.reason: Not a rehash — it is the repo's own recorded-but-unexecuted recommendation: memory feedback_recall_recovery_constraints.md (lines 36-41) explicitly names 'Splice / Copy-Paste of 补标 sub-arrays onto other roof patches, synthesizing multi-array roof training distribution' as a sanctioned training-time recall path, written in the same memo that killed all four inference-time recall tricks. Grep-verified zero copy-paste/mosaic code exists (train.py:244-385 claim accurate). It avoids the mechanism that sank both failed retrains (train20_val5_hn / jhb_phaseA died from SAM-halo boundary noise entering mask BCE; trusted-mask-only sourcing defuses that), and it does not touch the clean_gt eval lock, volume losses, or postproc. But the candidate as written has four repo-history defects that must be fixed: (1) its success criterion ('+1-2pp polygon F1') ignores the Tier-1 metric system and the dual-merge-mode rule — this repo has twice seen chip-level gains Goodhart-fail end-to-end (v4_2 AP50 0.8371→deploy F1 0.587; train20 chip F1 0.737→lost v4_canonical); (2) pasting onto negative-pool chips risks consuming the intentional hard-negative stream (CLAUDE.md rule 4, HN-breadth constraint d); (3) the negative pool is 678 manifest rows + PNG previews with no chip exporter (scripts/training/negative_pool/ has only bootstrap_from_cls_v2.py), so the C2 dependency is a hard prerequisite, not 'reuse'; trusted pool is 3,124 instances not 3.4k; (4) the proposed wiring point is structurally wrong — TrainTransforms.__call__(image, target) is single-sample, but copy-paste needs a cross-image source-instance bank at dataset/collate level, threading mask_pixel_weights/mask_weights/label_sources.
history.modification: Keep the core idea but rewrite four elements: (1) Success gate: replace '+1-2pp polygon F1' with the Tier-1 protocol — primary judges sigma_Bw + RMSE with bulk_ratio in [0.5,2.0] sanity gate on the locked JHB CBD25 clean_gt via area_aggregate_eval.py, plus Ch2 recall@{0.3,0.5} (the dense multi-array deficit this targets, currently 0.443 baseline) and cape_town_independent_26 presence F1 as secondary; evaluate the new checkpoint under BOTH merge modes (pixel-or and per-detection) per the first-class-lever rule. (2) Negative-stream preservation: paste only onto COPIES of negative-pool chips added as extra positive samples; the original empty-target hard-negative chips remain in the export at unchanged ratio. (3) Declare C2 (negative-pool chip exporter/ingest tooling) as a blocking prerequisite and correct pool sizes (3,124 trusted instances in data/training_pool/positive_trusted_manifest.csv). (4) Implementation: build the paste op as a dataset-level transform (source-instance bank sampled from the trusted manifest, GSD-matched per imagery layer) rather than inside TrainTransforms.__call__; pasted instances carry mask_trusted=true and a distinct provenance tag, and must thread mask_pixel_weights/mask_weights/label_sources through (the scale-jitter block at train.py:330-383 shows the required bookkeeping). (5) Train on the unified_reviewall recipe (mask_trusted gating + --freeze-mask-head staging + --seed), one lever per commit per the supervision-layering discipline — not a naive V3-C warm start, since both naive-recipe retrains failed end-to-end.

evidence.reason: Evidence transfers only partially. (a) Metric mismatch: Ghiasi +0.6-1.5 is COCO mask AP measured jointly with LSJ/long schedules on 118k images; Kisantal +9.7% is relative small-object AP (~+1pp absolute). Neither measures polygon F1@IoU0.5 against fragmented SAM-derived GT, and the claimed +1-2pp sits inside this project's noise band (merge-mode choice alone swings 6pp). (b) Direct in-project counter-evidence: real dense multi-array supervision (the SAM-supplemented 'gold-standard' corpus) already went into train20_val5_hn and Ch2 recall DROPPED 12pp with bulk +49pp — synthesizing more of the same configuration class only works if C2's trusted-mask gating fixes the mask-volume root cause, so C6 has no independent expected impact; it is conditional on C2. (c) The lookalike-FP half of the 'why' is redundant with the deployed solar_cls layer (kill 0.959, CT post-proc locked CLS-only; cross-domain lookalikes are a designated cls target), so half the claimed benefit is already extracted downstream. (d) Repo check confirms train.py has no copy-paste (claim verified) but the negative pool is a 678-row manifest, not chips — chips materialize at COCO-export time, and the 'trusted 3.4k pool' doesn't exist yet (C2 deliverable). Cost estimate omits provider-appearance matching (Kasmi: acquisition shift -0.11 F1 dominates GSD -0.02), which forces same-imagery-layer paste pairs and shrinks usable combinatorics. The defensible residue is the dense-multi-array-recall mechanism: it is the one untried training-time recall lever, consistent with the project's finding that inference-side recall fixes are exhausted, and was never considered/rejected in the supervision plan. Keep that, descoped and gated.
evidence.modification: Descope C6 to a dense-recall-only pilot, hard-sequenced behind C2: (1) Drop the symmetric negative-object pasting and the lookalike-FP claim entirely — FP suppression stays with solar_cls; negative-pool roofs serve only as optional paste canvases. (2) Paste sources exclusively from C2's volume-trusted mask pool (≤600m² halo-audit class, V3C_TP ∪ vetted SAM_supp); if C2's own retrain shows trusted masks still overshoot bulk_ratio, kill C6 without running it. (3) Constrain paste source and target canvas to the SAME imagery layer (appearance/provider match, not just GSD match per Kasmi). (4) Restate expected impact: primary metric = recall on the dense multi-array roof stratum (the 1/20-detected class from project_ch1_fragmentation / Ch2), with overall polygon F1 expected ±1pp (likely indistinguishable); pass gates = dense-stratum recall up by a pre-registered margin AND Tier-1 area metrics (bulk_ratio, σ_Bw) within noise of the C2 baseline, evaluated under BOTH merge modes. (5) Re-budget: 2-3 days for the transform (decide export-time compositing vs tensor-level transform, handling mask_pixel_weights) + 1 retrain + standard Ch2/Ch3 eval; explicit kill gate, no iteration on paste realism beyond alpha-blend in the pilot.

candidates.title: Optimization-recipe bundle: LR warmup + weight EMA + SWA + multi-scale training (min_size list)
candidates.what: In train.py: (1) add linear warmup (500-1000 iters) before the Stage-2 cosine (currently cosine starts cold after an optimizer swap, grep warmup = 0 hits); (2) add EMA of weights (decay 0.999) and select checkpoints on EMA weights; (3) after the main run, do a 6-12 epoch SWA pass with cyclical LR and average; (4) pass a min_size LIST (e.g. [640,800,960]) to the model transform for true multi-scale training (currently fixed 800 = silent 2x upsample of every 400px chip, single scale). All are recipe flags, no architecture change; one combined retrain is acceptable since these are non-interacting standard practice.
candidates.why: These are the zero-risk +1-2 AP class staples the stack lacks entirely (training auditor: no warmup, no EMA, no SWA, single fixed scale). EMA additionally reduces checkpoint-selection noise — directly relevant because chip-level F1@85 selection has repeatedly diverged from grid-level outcomes, and smoother weights shrink that variance. Multi-scale training is the retained half of the iSAID +7.8 bundle and doubles as mild GSD robustness for cross-layer deployment.
candidates.expected_impact: +1-2pp polygon F1 combined. Basis: Detectron2 zoo 1x->3x with scale aug = +2.0 mask AP; SWA-OD +1.0 AP across 6 detector families; EMA worth a few tenths in torchvision reference recipes.
candidates.cost: 2-3 days flags + 1 retrain at ~1.5x epochs; no annotation, no new data.
candidates.evidence: train.py:1413-1423 (cold cosine, no warmup); grep warmup/EMA = 0 hits (training auditor); arXiv:2012.12645 (SWA-OD); Detectron2 MODEL_ZOO 1x vs 3x.
candidates.risk: Longer schedules overfit ~10k-chip sets — keep early-eval every epoch + EMA selection; multi-scale changes the effective object-size distribution seen by fixed anchors (pairs naturally with C8's anchor audit); verify deployment thresholds re-swept since score distributions shift slightly.

history.reason: All in-repo factual claims verified correct (train.py:1413-1423 cold CosineAnnealingLR after fresh SGD swap, zero warmup/EMA/SWA hits anywhere in repo history, core/models/maskrcnn.py:76 builds maskrcnn_resnet50_fpn with torchvision-default fixed min_size=800 against 400px chips). Nothing in the 13-item rejected ledger covers optimizer-recipe changes; multi-scale train aug is not cross-imagery fusion. No constraint violation on clean_gt lock, volume loss, or HN breadth. However, the candidate fails on two repo-history grounds as written: (a) "one combined retrain since non-interacting" is false for multi-scale — it changes object-size distribution vs fixed anchors (candidate admits the C8 interaction) and effective GSD exposure, so bundling it with the optimizer tweaks repeats the exact Phase A bundling mistake that produced the codified one-lever-per-commit discipline; (b) the EMA justification misreads history — chip-F1@85 vs grid-level divergence in this repo (train20_val5_hn, v4_2) was systematic Goodhart from GT volume bias, not selection variance, so EMA cannot fix it and must not be sold that way. It also omits the mandatory dual-merge-mode Tier-1 gate (constraint g).
history.modification: Split into two gated runs and fix the gates. Run 1 (one retrain): warmup + EMA, with SWA as a post-hoc cyclical pass on the same job — these are post-hoc separable (raw-best, EMA-best, SWA-averaged = three checkpoints from one training job), preserving attribution without extra retrains. Train on the byte-identical unified_reviewall dataset manifest + seed (run-ledger from commit 8d93473) so the only delta vs unified_reviewall_A is the recipe. Run 2 (separate, gated on Run 1 >= baseline): multi-scale min_size list [640,800,960], explicitly merged with C8's anchor audit since the size-distribution interaction is admitted; thread min_size through core/models/maskrcnn.py:build_solar_maskrcnn (it currently exposes no such arg) and verify compatibility with the install_transform_aux_resize patch at train.py:1062 (inference builds its own model, so deployment resize stays 800). Pass gate for every checkpoint: Tier-1 (sigma_Bw + RMSE primary, bulk sanity) on JHB CBD25 clean_gt under BOTH merge modes plus polygon-conf re-sweep, baseline = unified_reviewall_A; chip-level F1@85/AP50 may pick candidates but may not declare the verdict. Rewrite the EMA rationale to claim only selection-variance reduction, dropping the implication that it addresses the chip-to-grid divergence.

evidence.reason: Factual code claims all check out (train.py:1413-1423 cold cosine, no warmup/EMA/SWA anywhere, core/models/maskrcnn.py uses torchvision default min_size=800 on 400px chips). But the evidence does not transfer at the claimed magnitude or cost: (a) all cited gains are COCO mask AP (IoU 0.5:0.95, 118k clean images, cold-start) — this project warm-starts from V3-C, already runs ~20 fine-tune epochs, and its headline metrics are polygon F1@0.5 plus area judges where mask gains are overwritten by production SAM refinement; (b) the project's own history (train20_val5_hn, JHB Phase A) shows chip-level recipe gains of exactly this size repeatedly failing grid-level gates, and SWA's extra epochs on noisy SAM-derived GT risk re-triggering the documented bulk-ratio overshoot; (c) "non-interacting" is false — torchvision uses min_size[-1] at eval, so a [640,800,960] list silently changes inference resize to 960 and breaks comparability with all existing results, plus multi-scale couples to the C8 anchor audit by the candidate's own admission; (d) cost omits the mandatory re-qualification chain (both merge modes, SAM reruns, per-layer conf re-sweep, solar_cls threshold recalibration, 25-grid Ch2/Ch3), roughly tripling the true cost. Only the warmup+EMA core survives as zero-risk hygiene.
evidence.modification: Strip the bundle to warmup + EMA only, implemented as train.py flags that piggyback on the NEXT already-planned retrain (no dedicated retrain for this candidate). Drop SWA entirely (extra-epoch averaging on ~10k noisy SAM-derived chips is contraindicated by the train20_val5_hn volume-overshoot failure; revisit only after GT cleanup). Move multi-scale min_size out of this candidate and into C8's anchor/scale audit as one joint experiment, with an explicit requirement to pin inference min_size=800 (torchvision eval uses min_size[-1]) so existing results stay comparable. Re-state expected impact as 0 to +1pp polygon F1 (selection-noise reduction, not AP-style gains) and add the full adoption gate to the cost line: both merge modes, SAM-refined variant, per-layer polygon-conf re-sweep, and solar_cls threshold recalibration before any production switch.

candidates.title: Anchor/proposal-budget audit and small-object retune (NOT NMS relaxation)
candidates.what: Step 1 (1 day, no GPU): histogram GT instance sizes in pixels at the model's working resolution (800px internal) per imagery layer from the COCO sets; audit anchor coverage (current: stock 32-512 x {0.5,1,2}, P5/P6 nearly dead) and measure per-chip detection counts vs detections_per_img=100 / rpn_post_nms_top_n=1000 truncation on dense chips (PTA-class). Step 2: if mismatch confirmed, set k-means-fit anchor sizes (likely adding 16px base or shifting to P2-heavy) in build_solar_maskrcnn() via anchor_generator override, raise box_detections_per_img to 300 and rpn_post_nms 2000 for inference, and retrain.
candidates.why: The model is pure-stock torchvision with anchors never fit to ~12x20px panel statistics at 7-9cm GSD (verified core/models/maskrcnn.py uses all defaults); the worst precision stratum (s_xs) and dense-roof recall are exactly where anchor mismatch and proposal-budget truncation bite. iSAID showed max-dets + multi-scale is the dominant aerial-specific config gain.
candidates.expected_impact: +0.5-2pp polygon F1, concentrated on s_xs and dense grids; possibly more if the histogram reveals sub-32px instances (DE anchor-tuning literature shows up to +14pp small-object AP as an upper bound; conservative +1-3 AP). The audit itself is free and decides whether to spend the retrain.
candidates.cost: 1 day audit (CPU); if triggered: 1-2 days code + 1 retrain. detections_per_img/rpn budget changes are inference-config-only (no retrain) and can ship immediately.
candidates.evidence: core/models/maskrcnn.py:76-83 stock build (verified); runtime anchor print (training auditor); arXiv:1905.12886 (iSAID Mask R-CNN+ +7.8 AP from max-dets/multi-scale/NMS); Sci Rep 2025 DE anchor optimization; ICEIC 2020 aerial anchor study.
candidates.risk: RESEMBLES REJECTED #4 (NMS relaxation) — material difference: the G0817 probe changed the NMS IoU threshold to rescue already-proposed-but-suppressed siblings and measured a no-op BECAUSE misses are proposal-stage; anchors and proposal/detection budgets act UPSTREAM at exactly the proposal stage the probe implicated. The NMS-threshold component of the iSAID bundle is explicitly excluded here. Residual risk: smaller anchors mint more small FPs (heater band) — co-deploy with C2 negatives and the cls filter; audit may show anchors are fine (cheap negative result).

history.reason: The core lever (anchor-fit audit) is genuinely untried — no anchor experiment exists anywhere in the repo, and the stock-build claim is verified (core/models/maskrcnn.py passes no anchor_generator/rpn/box overrides; 400px chips upscale to 800px internal per train.py:1058-1059). The NMS-relaxation distinction is factually sound: the G0817 probe's own verdict was that misses are proposal-stage failures fixable only by "TTA or a retrained model" — anchor refit is exactly such a retrained-model fix, so it complements rather than rehashes reject #4. However, the candidate contains one stale claim that must be corrected before it can survive: detect_direct.py has defaulted to detections_per_img=300 since the direct pipeline landed (commit 856ddd0; lines 97/230-231/284) — only the legacy geoai parity/benchmark path still runs 100 — so half of the "ship immediately" inference-config deliverable is already in production. The retrain half also underweights that changing anchor layout breaks RPN-head warm-start (a real retrain in a repo where 2/3 recent retrains failed on supervision grounds) and names no compliance with standing gates (b)/(g).
history.modification: Four concrete changes: (1) Strike "raise box_detections_per_img to 300 ... can ship immediately" — it is already the production default in detect_direct.py (300 since commit 856ddd0; 100 only in --parity-mode geoai). Replace with: audit truncation at the ACTUAL budgets per path — 300 on the direct/production path, 100 on the legacy geoai benchmark path — and if 100 binds on dense chips, file it as a benchmark-comparability bug for run_benchmark, not as a recall lever. (2) Gate the rpn_post_nms_top_n 1000→2000 bump on audit evidence of proposal saturation (measured proposal/detection counts on PTA-class dense chips), to stay consistent with the standing "TTA is the only sanctioned inference-time recall lever" verdict — no data, no ship. (3) If the histogram triggers a retrain: note that anchor-layout changes reinitialize the RPN head (no clean warm-start), make it a single-lever commit on the unified_reviewall supervision recipe (mask_trusted/boundary_w, untrusted<=4x trusted, freeze-mask-head staging), and judge it under the standing gates — dual merge-mode evaluation (constraint g) and the Tier-1 metric set with sigma_Bw+RMSE primary (constraint b) on the locked clean_gt — not on polygon F1 alone. (4) Re-anchor expected impact: GT instances are sub-arrays (clean_gt median ~26.8 m² ≈ ~150px at the 800px internal resolution), not 12x20px panels; drop the +14pp literature upper bound and present "anchors are adequate, audit closes the question for ~1 CPU-day" as the modal outcome, with +0.5-1pp on s_xs/dense strata as the optimistic branch.

evidence.reason: Both factual premises are refuted by repo-verifiable arithmetic, executed during this review. (1) Anchor mismatch: inference runs 400x400px chips (CHIP_SIZE in detect_and_evaluate.py) through the stock GeneralizedRCNNTransform(min_size=800), which upscales 2x — at the model's actual working resolution, sampled GT (n=392, CT+JHB) has sqrt-area p1=33-39px, median 137-164px, with 0.0-0.3% below the smallest 32px anchor; the candidate's "~12x20px" figure is native-GSD and ignores the internal upscale. The MIN_OBJECT_AREA=5m² postproc floor additionally deletes anything below ~45-67px working-res side, so sub-32px anchors target objects the chain discards or that feed the heater FP band. "P5/P6 nearly dead" is also false (p75-p99 = 290-1060px). (2) Budget truncation: box_detections_per_img=100 is top-k BY SCORE per ~27x27m chip; truncating a production-gate TP (c=0.925 per-det, or 0.85 tiered) requires 100 higher-scored detections in the same chip — the flagship dense-recall failure involves ~20 instances/roof, 5x below the cap and 50x below the 1000-proposal RPN budget, and the truncated low-conf tail dies at the conf/area/CLS gates anyway. (3) Evidence transfer fails on metric and architecture: iSAID's +7.8 AP is COCO-AP-with-maxDets on 4000px scenes with hundreds of instances (AP mechanically rewards low-conf tail detections; F1 at a high-conf operating point does not), and its largest component is multi-scale training which the candidate doesn't ship; the DE +14pp AP_S is one-stage RetinaNet where anchors ARE the hypotheses — two-stage RPN with allow_low_quality_matches guarantees every GT a positive anchor and is well-documented as anchor-insensitive. (4) The "+0.5-2pp concentrated on s_xs" claim is directionally backwards: s_xs x c_lo is the worst PRECISION stratum (0.294) and the top cross-domain failure mode is lookalike overpaint; raising small-object sensitivity worsens both. The candidate's last defense — "the audit is free and decides the retrain" — is voided because the audit's two decision-relevant measurements were just completed here (size histogram at working resolution + cap-binding arithmetic) and both return negative; spending a further day re-deriving them has no information value, and shipping the budget bump anyway would invalidate config.json result-cache comparability for a predicted no-op.

candidates.title: T1 gold installation-level evaluation suite + resident H anchor pool (new channel, clean_gt untouched)
candidates.what: Execute supervision-layering actions 6 + Phase B as one annotation campaign: RA freehand-verifies 150-250 installation-level polygons (QGIS, installation merge/boundary rules, A1/T1) stratified over the 6 failure archetypes (large >500m2, dense multi-array, shadowed, light roofs, V3C-halo-heavy, SAM-wobble like G0925), drawn from JHB CBD 25 + CT independent_26 areas. Use it as (a) a NEW eval suite 'ch2_t1_gold' in configs/benchmarks/ — re-score production checkpoints against T1 vs A2 GT on the SAME areas to measure the GT-noise ceiling directly; (b) a permanent high-weight training anchor pool (mask_weight 2.0, ignore 0) folded into builds, with the per-batch H-presence sampler (action 3, label_source plumbing already exists in train.py).
candidates.why: Zero freehand installation-level gold exists anywhere in training or eval (data auditor: 0 human_manual polygons in production; annotation_manifest covers 382 of 9,738). Without a T1 suite, the project cannot separate 'model wrong' from 'GT carved/noisy' — the literature shows that separation moves headline numbers massively (SolarMapper precision 0.45->0.67 purely from GT audit). The anchor pool is the planned cycle-breaker against pseudo-label drift.

candidates.cost: 20-40 RA annotation hours + 2-3 days suite/sampler wiring; no GPU beyond re-scoring.
candidates.evidence: docs/plans/2026-05-09-training-supervision-layering.md actions 3/6; docs/plans/2026-05-08-jhb-phaseA-retrain.md:183-189 (Phase B spec, never started); arXiv:1902.10895 (GT-audit precision swing); ANNOTATION_SPEC Two-Axis rules (T1 requires explicit human review).

Risks: annotator drift from installation rules (mitigate with the existing review webapp + spec checklist); small N gives wide CIs (report bootstrap CIs; 200 polygons gives ~+/-5pp on F1 — frame as ceiling-estimation, not model ranking).

history.reason: Not a rehash: Phase B (jhb-phaseA plan:183-189) and supervision-layering actions 3/6 are verified pending-never-started, and the superseded micro-T1 was model-initiated (V3C_TP∪SAM_supp), not freehand A1 — so a true T1 gold suite is genuinely novel and is the project's cheapest path to separating 'model wrong' from 'GT carved', which the eval-semantics evidence supports. Prerequisites mostly check out (train.py label_source plumbing confirmed at lines 28/58-105; manifest 382 rows with 0 human_manual confirmed). But the candidate has three repo-history violations/inaccuracies that must be fixed: (1) naming it 'ch2_t1_gold' collides with the clean_gt lock — feedback_eval_gt_lock_clean explicitly forbids ALL JHB-CBD-25 channels (Ch1/Ch2/Ch3) from drifting to micro-T1-style GT and warns one --gt-pattern flip causes silent drift; (2) the training half bundles actions 3+6+Phase-B mask_weight 2.0 in one campaign, repeating the exact Phase-A bundling mistake the plan forbids, and mask_weight=2.0 has no plumbing (boundary_trust_rules.yaml is strictly binary 1.0/0.0 — new per-instance weight code required); (3) installation-level anchors inside an ~80% sub-array-carved corpus (median 26.8 m², the documented train20 failure root cause) create conflicting instance labels on dense multi-array roofs — unaddressed. Also 248 A1/T1 sam-assisted polygons + cape_town_t1_smoke suite already exist (G1189/G1190/G1238), contradicting the 'zero T1 anywhere' framing.
history.modification: (1) Rename and reposition the eval suite: NOT 'ch2_t1_gold' — Ch2 stays locked to clean_gt. Make it a new standalone diagnostic channel (e.g. 'gtnoise_t1_ceiling') whose outputs carry an explicit gt_source=t1_gold field, are banned from model-ranking main tables, and never touch compute_ch2_recall/area_aggregate_eval defaults; all existing JHB CBD 25 channel numbers continue to be computed against clean_gt. Score the T1-vs-A2 rescoring with strict 1:1 polygon F1@IoU0.5 + the installation merge profile + Tier-1 area metrics under BOTH merge modes (presence F1@0.1 would mask the effect; constraint g requires dual-mode). (2) De-bundle the training half into three independently gated commits per the plan's own one-lever-per-commit rule: (a) anchor-pool fold-in using the EXISTING binary trusted mechanism (mask_trusted=true, boundary_w=1.0) with a baseline run; (b) per-batch H-presence sampler (action 3) as a separate ablation; (c) mask_weight=2.0 only after writing the missing per-instance weight plumbing, as its own ablation — do not ship all three in one retrain. (3) Add a granularity guard: dense multi-array-roof anchors must follow the spec's >1m-gap separation rule (separate systems = separate polygons), and chips containing an installation-level anchor must have overlapping sub-array A2 polygons either excluded or mask_trusted=false, so the same roof never carries conflicting-granularity supervision. evidence.reason: The eval-suite leg survives: the gold-GT gap is verified real (382 manifest rows all sam_assisted/blank, zero human_manual; even Capetown_Li is 17/17 T2 sam_assisted), the literature recommendation to audit GT and report the noise ceiling transfers (SolarMapper Findings 1-3, BDAPPV consensus filtering), the paired re-score design (same predictions, GT swapped) makes N=200 statistically workable despite the candidate's own ±5pp worry, and it is genuinely GPU-free and clean_gt-lock-safe. But two evidence-transfer failures require modification: (1) the headline SolarMapper 0.45→0.67 magnitude is a registry-parcel-matching audit, not polygon carving noise — it cannot anchor the +3-6pp claim, and failure-archetype stratification biases the measured T1-vs-A2 delta upward, so as designed the suite cannot report a corpus-wide GT-noise ceiling; (2) the training-anchor leg contradicts the candidate's own cited plan — installation-level merged polygons at mask_weight 2.0 are the explicitly rejected 'Sub-array → installation pre-merge' route ('不进 sprint…等效引入 dilation noise, inter-array gap 进 mask'), Phase B as written specifies clean-boundary sub-array hand-edits instead, the +0.5-1.5pp claim has no quantitative anchor and sits below the project's documented merge-mode/conf-sweep noise floor (~6pp), three prior small-set retrains all regressed, and the 'no GPU' cost claim is false for this leg since realizing it requires a retrain. candidates.title: Provider/appearance-shift augmentation: blur + wavelet/JPEG + cross-layer photometric (training-time, NOT inference fusion)
candidates.what: Extend TrainTransforms with acquisition-condition augmentation: Gaussian/defocus blur, JPEG compression artifacts, wavelet-domain perturbation (Kasmi's recipe), stronger color/illumination jitter, and mild GSD randomization (resample 0.8-1.5x then back) — calibrated so CT aerial chips occasionally LOOK like Vexcel/GEID texture statistics. Apply in the same retrain as C6/C7 or standalone; judge on xdomain60 sigma_Bw/per-city tables and GEID-layer suites, with in-domain non-regression gates.
candidates.why: Kasmi et al. show acquisition/provider shift (-0.11 F1) dominates resolution shift (-0.02) and geographic shift (~0) for PV models, and that the CNN's reliance on non-transferable finest-scale features is the mechanism — precisely matching ZAsolar's pattern (PMB/ELS fine, durban/bfn/gqeberha sigma_Bw blowup; GEID SSIM 0.21 'appearance not scale' per project memory). The 6-city census ambition makes per-grid dispersion the economic deliverable.
candidates.expected_impact: Cross-domain: sigma_Bw reduction on the worst cities and +1-2pp cross-domain polygon F1; in-domain ~0 (possibly -0.5pp, acceptable per gates). Basis: Kasmi's blur+wavelet recovered +0.02 absolute classification F1 cross-provider on a much easier task; the dispersion metric should respond more strongly than mean F1 since it is driven by tail grids.
candidates.cost: 2-3 days transforms + shares a retrain with C6/C7; no annotation.
candidates.evidence: Cambridge EDS Kasmi et al. (provider >> resolution shift; wavelet aug); memory project_geid_domain_gap (SSIM 0.21, appearance-not-scale); xdomain60 per-city sigma_Bw table.
candidates.risk: Distinct from REJECTED #3 (cross-imagery FUSION at inference — alignment-dependent); this is training-time appearance randomization, no imagery co-registration involved. Residual risk: over-aggressive degradation aug can soften thin-line features the small-panel detector needs (the GEID 'thin-line softening' problem) — sweep aug strength; gains capped by the hard GSD cliff (~30cm) the literature documents for GEID-class layers.

history.reason: The mechanism survives the rehash check: TrainTransforms (train.py:244-385) has only flips/rotations/mild color jitter/scale jitter — blur, JPEG, wavelet, and GSD resample-down-up augmentation have never been tried, and the candidate is genuinely distinct from rejected lever #3 (inference-time cross-imagery fusion; no co-registration involved here). It does not move the clean_gt goalpost and names Tier-1 metrics. But it fails as-specified on two counts. (1) Misattributed primary success criterion: xdomain60 is SAME-provider Vexcel 6.9-7.4cm (doc line 7), so the durban/bfn/gqeberha sigma_Bw blowup is geographic/city-texture shift — exactly the regime the candidate's own cited Kasmi result says appearance augmentation does ~nothing for. The repo's failure attribution assigns Mode-1 over-paint to lookalike FP (solar_cls + negative-pool archetype breadth territory, constraint d: same-type robustness scaling does not kill lookalike FP) and Mode-2 to dense recall collapse; neither is acquisition shift. Judged on xdomain60 sigma_Bw the lever will fail or steal credit from the cls attach. (2) The "same retrain as C6/C7" option repeats the Phase A bundling mistake (one-lever-per-commit discipline). Also: the extension point is flagged buggy (codebase-opt train-3, scale-jitter dead/wrong area re-index ternary at train.py:363) and aug code is duplicated with PhaseA transforms (train-2).
history.modification: Keep the augmentation recipe but re-scope the evaluation and execution: (1) Primary judge becomes the true cross-provider surface: run the augmented checkpoint on geid_2024_02 (still registered in regions.yaml) over the same JHB CBD 25 grids against the same locked clean_gt, and score the provider-shift delta (GEID vs vexcel_2024) on Tier-1 metrics — this isolates exactly the SSIM-0.21 appearance gap the lever targets without moving any GT goalpost. (2) In-domain non-regression gates on cape_town_independent_26 and clean_gt-Vexcel, evaluated under BOTH merge modes (constraint g), with the seeded (--seed/--deterministic) baseline rerun since claimed deltas are 1-2pp scale. (3) Demote xdomain60 per-city sigma_Bw to secondary/observational only, scored AFTER solar_cls is attached cross-domain, so augmentation is not credited/blamed for lookalike-FP variance it cannot affect. (4) Run as a standalone retrain — drop the "same retrain as C6/C7" option (Phase A one-lever-per-commit lesson). (5) Before adding GSD randomization, fix the train-3 scale-jitter area re-index bug (train.py:363) and resolve the train-2 aug duplication so the new transforms land once.

evidence.reason: The candidate's evidence does not transfer because its mechanism is aimed at a shift this project does not have on its live judges. (1) Mechanism mismatch: regions.yaml + docs/experiments/2026-05-31-xdomain60-unified-reviewall-A.md confirm all six xdomain60 cities are Vexcel 2025-26 ortho at ~6.9-7.4cm — same provider family and near-identical GSD as the training vexcel_2024 layer. That is a geographic shift, which Kasmi (the candidate's sole quantitative anchor) measures at ~0 F1 cost; his -0.11 provider-shift finding and blur/wavelet remedy address a gap that does not exist on the candidate's own primary judge (xdomain60 sigma_Bw per-city tables). The project's experiment doc attributes the 8x sigma_Bw blowup to lookalike-FP over-paint (durban/bfn, explicitly the solar_cls classifier's target, deliberately unattached in that baseline) and dense-roof recall collapse (pta) — semantic/distributional failures, with PMB/ELS on identical Vexcel imagery generalizing near in-domain, which directly falsifies an appearance-shift explanation. (2) The one real appearance gap (CT aerial vs Vexcel) is already covered by stronger evidence than augmentation: unified_reviewall_v2 trains on real mixed-provider data (5,903 CT aerial_2025 + 3,835 JHB vexcel_2024 polygons). The GEID gap (SSIM 0.21) is real but GEID is a retired detector surface — JNB Vexcel-382 is canonical and backdating scores historical chips with Gemini, not the detector — so the proposed GEID-suite judge tests a non-deployment. (3) Novelty is thinner than claimed: TrainTransforms in train.py already includes color jitter (brightness/contrast +/-0.2, saturation +/-0.15, p=0.8) and 0.8-1.2x scale jitter; the genuinely new delta is blur/JPEG/wavelet, whose source payoff is +0.02 absolute on chip-level binary classification F1 (recovering ~18% of an 11pp provider drop) — the claimed +1-2pp polygon F1@IoU0.5 exceeds the entire source gain after transfer to a much harder metric, and the 'sigma_Bw responds more strongly' claim is unsupported speculation given the tail grids are driven by Mode 1/2 failures the aug cannot touch. (4) Asymmetric risk: degradation aug softens thin-line/small-panel features on the s_xs stratum where Ch1 precision is already worst (0.294), and the team's retrain history (train20, JHB Phase A, zerov2 Phase 0) shows in-domain regressions are the norm — spending a shared retrain slot plus a 60-grid 6-city re-inference on a mechanism the project's own data has localized to semantics, not photometrics, is negative expected value. Revival condition: only if a future pipeline actually points the Mask R-CNN detector at a genuinely different provider (e.g., CoJ ArcGIS historical aerial or GEHI-class imagery for a 2023 census), reintroduce blur/JPEG as a zero-cost rider on whatever retrain that work requires.

candidates.title: Score calibration: per-imagery-layer temperature scaling + optional MaskIoU (mask-scoring) head
candidates.what: Phase 1 (no retrain): fit per-imagery-layer temperature/Platt calibration of detection confidence on small held-out grid sets (NOT the reporting grids), inserted in finalize.py before tiered-conf filtering, so one locked operating point transfers across layers/cities instead of requiring per-suite GT-tuned sweeps. Also fix the documented legacy-path bug where post_conf from --postproc-config is silently ignored in favor of hardcoded CONF_TIERED (detect_and_evaluate.py:798). Phase 2 (retrain, optional): add a MaskIoU head (Mask Scoring R-CNN) so confidence reflects mask quality.
candidates.why: The CT-census wave1 ranking FLIPPED with threshold protocol, and headline numbers depend on post-hoc sweeps on the same grids that produce the number (eval auditor) — miscalibrated raw scores are the root cause. Calibrated scores raise F1 at FIXED protocol (the F1-optimal threshold becomes findable without GT), and de-risk cross-domain deployment where sweeps are impossible.
candidates.expected_impact: +0.5-1.5pp polygon F1 at fixed honest protocol, plus elimination of ranking-flip ambiguity worth ~6pp in historical verdicts; MaskIoU adds ~+1.5 AP per literature if pursued.
candidates.cost: Phase 1: 2-3 days, no GPU; Phase 2: small head + retrain (~1-2 GPU-days).
candidates.evidence: memory project_ct_census_threshold_tradeoff (ranking flips with threshold caliber); detect_and_evaluate.py:798 CONF_TIERED bug (eval auditor); arXiv:1903.00241 (Mask Scoring +1.5 AP); feedback_postconf_polygon_sweep.
candidates.risk: Calibration sets must be disjoint from reporting suites or it reintroduces sweep leakage; per-layer calibration data is thin for new cities (fall back to global temperature); consistent with constraint c — this manages conf-noise, not structural FP, and claims accordingly.

history.reason: The candidate's core motivation is stale and its Phase 1 is a mathematical near-no-op against current practice.
