(3) De-bias the success criterion: judging "match or beat V3-C+SAM without SAM" on clean_gt alone structurally favors the SAM incumbent; require the Li GT independent cross-check and the dense-roof failure set (G0817/G0925 class, where GT was human-corrected) in the decision table, both merge modes. (4) Reset expected impact: delete the +1-2pp polygon F1@0.5 claim; state the realistic envelope as at-or-below the COCO +1.1 mask-AP figure concentrated above IoU 0.75, with the defensible win being SAM-stage removal (pipeline simplification + escape from roof-swallow on large contiguous PV), not headline metric gains.

reportsSummary.read:training: ZAsolar's training stack is a completely stock torchvision maskrcnn_resnet50_fpn (core/models/maskrcnn.py:76-83): default anchors ((32..512) x (0.5,1,2)), default RPN/ROI params (1000 post-NMS proposals, 100 detections/image), default 28x28 mask head, and a fixed min_size=800 internal resize that silently 2x-upsamples every 400-px chip at both train and inference — no anchor tuning to the ~0.074-0.089 m GSD panel statistics anywhere in the repo. Augmentation (train.py:244-385) is flips + rot90 + mild photometric jitter + 0.8-1.2 top-left-anchored scale jitter; there is no copy-paste, mosaic, random crop, large-scale jitter, or true multi-scale training. Optimization is two-stage SGD (3 ep heads @1e-3, then up to 20 ep full @1e-4 cosine), AMP, grad-clip 10, batch 32, with no warmup, no EMA/SWA, and checkpoint selection on chip-level F1@conf0.85 (greedy IoU0.5) — grid-level area_F1/bulk metrics are post-hoc only and have historically diverged from this proxy. Where the project is ahead of baseline is supervision hygiene: --freeze-mask-head, --reinit-mask-head/box-predictor, differential LR, and a per-source mask-trust system (per-instance mask BCE gating + per-pixel boundary-band ignore via a patched maskrcnn_loss) to stop V3-C halo feedback; however, ignore support never reaches RPN/box-cls loss and is unreachable from the COCO dataset path, so unlabeled/ambiguous panels train as hard background. Sampling is plain shuffle — negative balance (0.15 easy-neg ratio + accumulating HN pool) is fixed at COCO export time, with no in-loop class-balanced or repeat-factor sampling. Inference (geoai legacy pixel-OR painter or detect_direct+finalize with per-detection/pixel-or merge, tiered conf/area/elongation filters, optional SAM mask+box refinement) has zero TTA, zero multi-scale, and zero score calibration; grep confirms none exist, and project memory already identifies TTA as the only viable inference-time recall lever. Highest-leverage missing items relative to Detectron2/MMDet-era practice: copy-paste + large-scale-jitter augmentation, anchor/detections-per-image tuning for dense small objects, multi-scale+flip TTA, higher-resolution mask supervision (56x56/PointRend) to attack the halo/boundary ceiling, EMA+warmup, and full ignore regions; additionally, part of the 0.75-0.81 vs 0.85-0.95 literature gap is metric semantics (pixel/panel-level F1 on clean benchmarks vs installation-level polygon F1 against A2 SAM-assisted GT under domain shift) rather than model capacity.
reportsSummary.read:data: The production detector (unified_reviewall_A) trains on 9,738 positive polygons across just 2 cities and 2 imagery layers (CT aerial_2025: 5,903 polygons / 68 grids; JHB vexcel_2024: 3,835 / 25 grids), yielding 9,819 train chips (8,539 positive + 1,280 easy negatives at neg_ratio=0.15) of 400x400 px with 0.16 overlap — and zero targeted hard-negative chips were bundled this round, since the 678-row negative_pool manifest has no geometry and the v4_3 HN pool (866 chips) was not merged; FP suppression rides entirely on V3-C warm-start weights. Supervision provenance is the core weakness for closing the F1 gap: 62.7% of train instances (5,691) are model-derived (reviewed_prediction / batch-SAM FN cuts) whose masks are excluded from BCE via mask_trusted gating, so the mask head effectively learns boundaries from only ~3,384 instances, and even those are 100% SAM-drawn sub-array polygons (A2) — there are zero freehand, installation-level gold masks in training. T1/T2 tier filtering exists in export_coco_dataset.py but is inactive in practice (annotation_manifest.csv covers only 382 annotations, 248 T1). The repo's own docs record four structural data weaknesses: (1) SAM/reviewed-prediction halo volume bias (pixel-BCE learns inflated boundaries; train20 bulk +49pp, Phase A bulk 1.897, both retrains failed); (2) sub-array fragmentation (one installation = several GT polygons, ~1.49:1 in batch-003 stats, pre-merge explicitly rejected); (3) lookalike-negative diversity loss (train20 unique FPs = roof textures/skylights; xdomain60 Mode-1 over-paint, sigma_Bw 8x degradation cross-domain); (4) uncorrected imagery anisotropy (CT 7.14x9.06 cm vs JHB 6.7 cm) plus the wholesale archiving of all aerial_2023 positives. Against literature that reports 0.85-0.95 F1 from 10k-100k+ installations over 50+ cities with neg:pos ratios of 1:1-32:1, ZAsolar sits at ~8.4k annotated installations total, ~3.4k trusted mask instances, 2 cities, and a 0.18:1 chip-level negative ratio that the repo's own literature review calls nearly the lowest in the field. The actionable data levers already identified internally are: raise neg_ratio to 0.5-0.75, backfill negative_pool geometry and broaden archetypes across regions/domains, build a small freehand installation-level gold anchor set (the 2026-05-09 plan's 100-200 polygon H-anchor pool and selective relabelling pipeline remain unexecuted), and expand positive-domain breadth beyond 2 imagery layers.
reportsSummary.read:eval: The project has no single 'polygon F1'; the quoted 0.75-0.81 band conflates three metrics. The benchmark's ranking F1 (run_benchmark.py reading presence_metrics.csv) is polygon-count F1 computed by greedy matching with pred-side many-to-one union merge at IoU>=0.1 (detect_and_evaluate.py:2097-2101) — extremely lenient localization; v3c scores ~0.709 on cape_town_independent_26 under it. The widely-quoted 0.800 for unified_reviewall_A is NOT polygon F1 at IoU 0.5 — it is pixel-level set-theoretic area F1 (area_aggregate_eval.py unary_union intersection/union) on 5 JHB Vexcel val grids at a polygon-conf=0.85/min_area=20 operating point chosen by sweeping on those same grids; true polygon recall at IoU>=0.5 on the 25-grid clean_gt is 68.8% (V3-C+SAM) to 79.6% (train20 per-det+SAM), and the strict 1:1 IoU 0.5 F1 used cross-domain is acknowledged as structurally depressed by sub-array-carved GT. Between raw Mask R-CNN output and evaluation sits a heavy postproc stack: mask_thr 0.3, pixel-or or per-detection merge (a ~6pp F1 lever), RGB shadow/over-bright filter, area>=5 m², tiered elongation (<=8 / <=15), tiered confidence (0.85/0.65/0.70 — hardcoded in the legacy path so config post_conf 0.92 is silently ignored), spatial NMS at IoU 0.5, plus optional SAM2 mask+box refinement, polygon-conf filtering, and solar_cls classifier filtering that the benchmark does NOT apply. Contamination controls are real (independent_26 excluded from training, leakage_risk tags, batch003 diagnostic excluded from ranking) but the JHB 25-grid clean_gt is partially built from accepted V3-C predictions, inflating V3-C-lineage numbers. Versus literature: the metric is harder because it is instance-level matching over full census grids saturated with lookalikes (water heaters etc.) against fragmented sub-array A2 GT on small objects, often cross-domain; it is easier because of the IoU-0.1 merge presence criterion, FP/FN cancellation in area F1, GT circularity, and post-hoc operating-point sweeps on the eval grids themselves. The honest literature comparison is the project's pixel-level area F1 (0.80-0.85 in-domain), which already sits at the bottom edge of the literature's 0.85-0.95 pixel-F1 range, meaning most of the perceived gap is metric-population mismatch rather than raw segmentation deficit; V1.4 has officially demoted all polygon F1 to diagnostic in favor of sigma_Bw + RMSE.
reportsSummary.read:results: The project's polygon-F1 ledger tops out at 0.709 on the canonical CT independent_26 suite (V3-C, P 69.6/R 72.2) and ~0.68-0.69 polygon@IoU0.5 on the JHB CBD 25-grid clean_gt for the V3-C lineage; the only run reaching ~0.80 polygon recall is train20 per-detection (79.3% @IoU0.5) which over-paints area (bulk 1.44), and the unified_reviewall_A 'F1=0.800' headline is a pixel-union area F1 at a swept operating point, not polygon F1. The gap versus literature 0.85-0.95 decomposes into four measured components: (1) GT-semantics mismatch — all CT/Li GT is SAM2 sub-array A2, so installation-level predictions are structurally penalized (same predictions score polygon F1 0.36 but area F1 0.76 cross-domain); (2) precision loss concentrated in one stratum — s_xs × c_lo precision 0.286 vs 0.78-1.00 elsewhere, with 77% of small FPs being solar water heaters in the 5-20m² band (32% FP rate) — now largely addressable by the solar_cls adaptive_v1 filter (kill 0.959 in-domain CT); (3) recall loss concentrated on sub-arrays of multi-array roofs (Ch2 SAM_supp recall 0.30 @IoU0.5; mid-size 30-100m² bucket 0.33) and split between postproc-recoverable (15-27% on G0816/G0817) and detector-bounded (G0925 mask undersizing, training-time fix only); (4) operating-point/postproc artifacts worth 5-13pp F1 (merge mode, polygon-conf sweep). Cross-domain, aggregate calibration holds (bulk 1.04, R² 0.97) but sigma_Bw degrades 8x with two distinct modes: lookalike over-paint on sparse grids (classifier target) and dense-roof recall collapse (PTA0292 area-R 0.06, training-bounded). Every attempted training fix to date (V4/V4.1 HN scaling, train20 clean-GT retrain, phaseA boundary-aware, frozen zerov2 Phase 0) either traded recall for precision or failed outright on sub-array-granularity supervision contamination; the standing path is the supervision-layering plan plus TTA for recall. Current production: CT census = unifiedA per-det + solar_cls adaptive_v1 (cov50 0.829, sigma_Bw 0.260); JHB = unified_A per-det+SAM @c=0.925 + Gemini FP-cut (41,393 polygons delivered).
reportsSummary.read:failed-experiments: The project keeps a dense rejected ledger: two full retrains failed and froze the supervision strategy (train20_val5_hn: Ch2 recall -12.2pp, bulk +49.3pp from 80.3% sub-array SAM_supp GT; jhb_phaseA boundary-aware retrain: bulk 1.897 / recall 0.300 / area_F1 0.608, all three pass criteria failed), and the 2026-05-09 supervision-layering plan codifies 7 do-not-re-challenge rejects (56x56 mask head, sub-array->installation pre-merge, PVSAM variant, boundary-aware loss, point supervision, size-aware backbone, pseudo-label BCE hard retraining) plus postproc rejects (per-detection-as-area-path for V3-C, envelope-group fill, stage3b-no-NMS, adaptive hysteresis) and all inference-side recall fixes except TTA (threshold+cls, rescan templating, cross-imagery, NMS relax all dead). Of the same plan's 7 priority actions, only 2 are fully executed (--freeze-mask-head in train.py:864 and the untrusted<=4x-trusted accumulation assertion in build_unified_reviewall.py:523-530); action 4 landed in a degraded binary form (mask_trusted + boundary_w 1.0/0.0 via boundary_trust_rules.yaml, no graded S=0.5), while actions 3 (per-batch H stratified sampler), 5 (standalone area-adaptive ignore-band module), 6 (resident H anchor pool) and 7 (selective relabelling) are entirely unexecuted despite prerequisites (label_source plumbing, clean_gt corpus, refine webapp) being in place. The highest-leverage open levers for closing the F1 gap are: TTA (the only sanctioned recall path, zero code exists), the four pending supervision actions plus the <=600m2 SAM_added staged-training pool (gate prerequisite done, pool never wired), PointRend/DynaMask/meta-reweighting in the mid-term backlog, and the approved-but-untouched solar_zerov2 diagnostic ladder whose Phase 0 frozen-backbone pilot lost to V3-C raw with unfreezing literally unimplemented. Cross-domain, the xdomain60 8x sigma_Bw degradation has two named unexecuted fixes (attach solar_cls cross-domain; dense-roof recall/domain adaptation), and negative-pool breadth expansion (ingest_fp_audit.py and the HN exporter unbuilt, durban/bloemfontein FP archetypes not ingested) remains open. Validation-side, CT census still lacks grid_strata.csv, Ch1 RA-precision tooling, CT Ch2 exhaustive GT, and external_agreement_grid.py; smaller pending items include v4_poly_diag.json and dual-merge-mode emission in validate_checkpoint.py, both explicitly recommended in the finalizer decision doc and never landed. A key caveat for the F1-gap narrative: the 2026-05-14 fair comparison showed the 'failed' train20 checkpoint wins Tier-1 under its native per-detection+SAM operating point, demonstrating that merge-mode/postproc caliber moves reported F1 by ~6-13pp and several historical verdicts were postproc artifacts rather than model deficits.
reportsSummary.read:zerov2: solar_zerov2 bets on frozen DINOv3-SAT-L/16 (timm vit_large_patch16_dinov3.sat493m, 303M params, MAXAR ~0.6m-GSD SSL pretrain) + ViTDet simple_fpn adapter (13.6M trainable) + HF Mask2Former decoder (33.5M, 300 queries, 1 class, stride-4 masks), trained CT-only (unified_reviewall_ct_only_chip512, 7227 chips of reviewed/human/SAM A2 annotations, all 25 JHB eval grids excluded) on 512px chips at native 6.7cm Vexcel GSD. The Phase 0 pilot (30 epochs, cached frozen features, EMA off, decoder random-init per the log, ~25h RTX 5090) scored 2026-05-29 on JHB CBD 25 clean_gt: F1 0.747/sigma_Bw 0.613 raw, 0.764/0.327 at best sweep c=0.95 — losing to the V3-C raw floor (0.817/0.248) and falsifying the set-prediction-lowers-variance thesis; failure is lookalike-FP over-painting on sparse grids (pred/GT up to 4.03). The result is confounded four ways: the GSD mitigation (scale_jitter) was inert, backbone unfreeze is unimplemented (freeze/lr_backbone config keys unread; cached mode deletes the backbone), only one adapter variant ran, and CT->JHB transfer was baked in. Governance: Codex v1 cross-review returned 24 holes/REPLAN MAJOR (verified blockers included 90.8% eval-grid training leakage and a nonexistent training set); R0 preflight B1-B10 all passed, Codex v2 re-escalated on 3 performative closures, and R0.5 + v3 closures landed an executable r1_unlock_check preflight; remaining open items are accepted risks V1.a/V3.a/V5.a, eval.py boundary-IoU/size-bucket implementation, attention-map renders, and the unfreeze path itself. Round 1 (4 cells, ~98 of 400 authorized A100-hours) stays locked until the pilot lineage clears the V3-C raw floor (F1>=0.817 AND sigma_Bw<=0.248). The minimal decisive next step is the repo's own Stage-1 cached probes 1b (web DINOv3-L, zero code, ~6h on one 5090) plus 1c (SAT at 13.4cm downsample, small caching change) — together ~1 GPU-day — which cleanly separate the pretrain-domain vs GSD-gap hypotheses; only if both stay below F1 0.78 does the expensive Stage-2 last-4-block unfreeze (~5 code changes, 1-2 GPU-days, CT26 overfit guard) become make-or-break. For the parent's F1-gap question: this architecture currently trails the V3-C lineage rather than leading it, so it is a cheap keep-or-kill option, not a near-term path to literature-level 0.85-0.95 F1.
reportsSummary.lit:sota-survey: Surveyed 2021-2026 rooftop-PV detection literature (DeepSolar lineage, Kasmi/BDAPPV/DeepPVMapper, SolarDK, Duke/SolarMapper, PV01/03/08, Kruitwagen, S3Former/SolarFormer, SAM-era 2024-26). The 0.85-0.95 'F1' figures are almost universally pixel-level F1/IoU on in-domain splits of curated chip datasets (USGS CA 30cm: pixel F1 0.94; PV01 China: 0.91; Heilbronn: 0.96) or image-level chip classification (DeepSolar P93/R89), with consensus-filtered GT and large-array bias — no major paper reports polygon/instance F1@IoU0.5. The best calibration anchor is the Duke SolarMapper 'pitfalls' study, which reports both levels on one system: pixel IoU 0.73 (CT) / 0.60 (San Diego) maps to array-wise AP@IoU0.5 of 0.82 / 0.71, bracketing ZAsolar's 0.80 polygon F1. On true aerial orthophotos the published SOTA pixel numbers collapse to ZAsolar's regime anyway: S3Former gets pixel F1 0.74/IoU 0.59 on IGN France aerial (vs 0.94 on USGS chips), and SolarDK's best is pixel IoU 0.72. Cross-domain drops in the literature exceed ZAsolar's: classification F1 0.98 to 0.46 across providers (Kasmi), Germany-to-Denmark zero-shot F1 0.238, DeepSolar-Germany precision 92 to 64 across sensors, SolarMapper CT-to-SD -13pp pixel IoU even with symmetric fine-tuning. GT noise is quantified as a first-class limiter: 42% of registry 'FPs' and 67% of 'FNs' were actually correct predictions (precision 0.45 to 0.67 after audit), BDAPPV crowd masks correlated only 0.61-0.68 with declared areas before consensus filtering, and human annotators miss ~3.8% of visible panels. Net: ZAsolar's F1 'gap' is a metric-type and dataset-curation artifact, not a model deficit; national-scale systems (DeepPVMapper, DeepSolar, SolarMapper) all validate via aggregate capacity/registry correlation (r 0.76-0.89, MRE ~3%), which directly endorses the V1.4 aggregate-inventory framing with per-polygon F1 as diagnostic.
reportsSummary.lit:maskrcnn-upgrades: Web research compiled the proven Mask R-CNN upgrade menu for aerial small-object instance segmentation with measured gains. Headline context first: the 0.85-0.95 F1 figures in PV literature are almost entirely pixel-level semantic-segmentation F1 on in-domain splits (U-Net 91.75%, HRNet 95.54%), while the only published instance-level PV delineation F1 is ~0.73 (satellite) / 0.68 (UAV) with Mask R-CNN — so ZAsolar's 0.75-0.81 polygon F1 already matches or beats the instance-level state of the art, and much of the perceived gap is metric semantics. The largest remote-sensing-specific documented gain is iSAID's 'Mask R-CNN+': +7.8 AP purely from raising max-detections 100->1000, six-scale augmentation, and NMS 0.5->0.6. Inference-only levers ranked: TTA multi-scale+flip (+1-2 mask AP, 4-8x cost, aligns with ZAsolar's TTA-only recall constraint), SAHI-style overlap-merge slicing (+5-7 AP small objects on VisDrone/xView), and F1-optimal per-layer threshold sweeps (already partially exploited internally). Retraining levers ranked by gain/cost: anchor re-tuning for small objects (up to +14 pp small-object AP in one DE-optimization study, conservatively +1-5), 3x schedule + multi-scale training (+2.0 mask AP per Detectron2 zoo), SWA (+1.0 AP free per Zhang 2020), copy-paste augmentation (+0.6-1.5 AP, +9.7% relative on small objects per Kisantal, synergistic with the negative pool), and backbone swap R50->Swin-T/ConvNeXt-T (+3.6-4.2 AP under identical recipe — the biggest single lever but overlapping the solar_zerov2 bet). Boundary-quality heads (PointRend +1.1-2.8, RefineMask +2.6, Transfiner +3.0/+6.6 Boundary AP) mainly improve AP75/boundary metrics, i.e. ZAsolar's bulk_ratio/sigma_Bw area judges rather than F1@0.5, and could replace SAM refinement; Mask Scoring R-CNN (+1.5 AP) improves the score calibration the threshold sweep depends on. Cascade/HTC heads are explicitly the wrong lever for IoU-0.5 polygon F1 (+0.8 AP50 only; gains concentrate at IoU>=0.75). GSD evidence sets a hard ceiling: PV is undetectable above ~30 cm GSD and model-capacity advantages only materialize at fine GSD, implying the cross-domain dispersion problem is resolution/domain-bound rather than fixable by any single architecture upgrade.
reportsSummary.lit:foundation-models: Survey of 2023-2026 foundation-model PV segmentation literature yields four decision-relevant facts. (1) The literature's 0.85-0.95 F1 figures are pixel-level semantic metrics on curated, PV-dense, in-domain chips (PV03, BDAPPV, multi-resolution sets — Mask2Former >95% IoU, PVSAM >90% F1/IoU); no surveyed PV paper reports open-world instance/polygon-level F1 over full city grids, and the closest instance-level RS analog (RSPrompter, WHU buildings) sits at 60-70 mask AP — so ZAsolar's 0.75-0.81 polygon F1 is largely a metric/setting difference plus A2 GT noise, not an architecture deficit. (2) Frozen geospatial foundation models do not reliably beat supervised CNNs (PANGAEA: best frozen GFM ~+0.9 mIoU over U-Net on SpaceNet7), fine-tuning the ViT backbone is worth up to +11.9 mIoU (DINO Soars), and at sub-10m GSD web-pretrained backbones beat satellite-pretrained ones (GEO-Bench-2) — together these triply predict the frozen DINOv3-SAT Phase 0 loss, and the decisive cheap ablation is unfreeze + swap to web-pretrained DINOv3 weights at native GSD. (3) GSD mismatch is real and large (CROMA -59% relative mIoU when resampled to pretrain resolution; Prithvi -40-47pp on-orbit), resampling to match pretrain GSD is harmful, and scale-aware encodings (Scale-MAE) recover only 0.9-3.8 mIoU; within fine-GSD aerial regimes, provider/acquisition shift dominates pure resolution (Kasmi: -0.11 F1 provider vs -0.02 resolution), pointing blur/wavelet appearance augmentation at the 6-city dispersion problem. (4) Label-efficiency gains from SSL/foundation pretraining concentrate below ~10% labels; at a few thousand polygons the curve is saturated (SimCLR adds <1pp IoU), and SAM-based weak supervision reaches ~88% of fully-supervised performance — useful for expanding cities, not for raising the in-domain ceiling. The strongest evidence-backed bet: fine-tuned web-pretrained DINOv2/v3 + Mask2Former head (full FT in-domain, Rein-style ~1%-parameter PEFT for cross-domain checkpoints, which beats full FT on domain generalization), SAM kept as boundary refiner/pseudo-labeler, and the existing semantic classifier layer retained since reasoning-based filters show the smallest cross-region degradation (PVAL -0.03-0.04 F1). Expected payoff is single-digit polygon-F1 points in-domain but a materially larger win on cross-city dispersion.
